channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Krebs on Security, The Hacker News, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading

item.title: First VPN Dismantled in Global Takedown Over Use by 25 Ransomware Groups
item.description: Authorities in Europe and North America have announced the dismantling of a criminal virtual private network (VPN) service used by criminal actors to obscure the origins of ransomware attacks, data th...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Netherlands seizes 800 servers of hosting firm enabling cyberattacks
item.description: Financial crime investigators in the Netherlands (FIOD) arrested two men and seized 800 servers linked to a web hosting company that enabled cyberattacks, interference operations, and disinformation c...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
item.description: Lawmakers in both houses of Congress are demanding answers from the U.S. Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) after KrebsOnSecurity reported this week that a CISA contractor intention...
item.dc:creator: Krebs on Security

item.title: Ghostwriter Targets Ukraine Government Entities with Prometheus Phishing Malware
item.description: The Belarus-aligned threat actor known as Ghostwriter (aka UAC-0057 and UNC1151Ukraine's National Security and Defense Council) has been observed using lures related to Prometheus, a Ukrainian online ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Former US execs plead guilty to aiding tech support scammers
item.description: Two former executives of a call-tracking and analytics company pleaded guilty to concealing a years-long tech support fraud scheme that victimized individuals worldwide. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Trend Micro warns of Apex One zero-day exploited in the wild
item.description: Japanese cybersecurity software company Trend Micro has addressed an Apex One zero-day vulnerability exploited in attacks targeting Windows systems. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Drupal: Critical SQL injection flaw now targeted in attacks
item.description: Drupal is warning that hackers are attempting to exploit a "highly critical" SQL injection vulnerability announced earlier this week. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Why Chargebacks are Just One Piece of the Fraud Puzzle
item.description: Fraud losses don't stop at chargebacks. False declines, account takeovers, and abuse also damage revenue and trust. IPQS breaks down why fraud teams need broader visibility into risk and customer impa...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Ubiquiti patches three max severity UniFi OS vulnerabilities
item.description: Ubiquiti has released security updates to patch three maximum severity vulnerabilities in UniFi OS that can be exploited by remote attackers without privileges. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Megalodon GitHub Attack Targets 5,561 Repos with Malicious CI/CD Workflows
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new automated campaign called Megalodon that has pushed 5,718 malicious commits to 5,561 GitHub repositories within a six-hour window.
"Using thr...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Making Vulnerable Drivers Exploitable Without Hardware - The BYOVD Perspective
item.description:
1 Introduction
This article provides a technical analysis of how many Windows kernel mode drivers can be interacted with from user mode without the hardware they were developed for. This work was mot...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: US and Canada arrest and charge suspected Kimwolf botnet admin
item.description: U.S. and Canadian authorities arrested and charged a Canadian man with operating the KimWolf distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) botnet, which infected nearly two million devices worldwide. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Kimwolf DDoS Botnet Operator Arrested in Canada Over DDoS-for-Hire Attacks
item.description:
The U.S. Department of Justice (DoJ) on Thursday announced the arrest of a Canadian man in connection with allegedly operating a distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) botnet known as Kimwolf.
In tande...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: CISA Adds Exploited Langflow and Trend Micro Apex One Vulnerabilities to KEV
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Thursday added two security flaws impacting Langflow and Trend Micro Apex One to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Cisco Patches CVSS 10.0 Secure Workload REST API Flaw Enabling Data Access
item.description:
Cisco has rolled out updates for a maximum-severity security flaw impacting Secure Workload that could allow an unauthenticated, remote attacker to access sensitive data.
Tracked as CVE-2026-20223 (C...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Alleged Kimwolf Botmaster ‘Dort’ Arrested, Charged in U.S. and Canada
item.description: Canadian authorities on Wednesday arrested a 23-year-old Ottawa man on suspicion of building and operating Kimwolf, a fast spreading Internet-of-Things botnet that enslaved millions of devices for use...
item.dc:creator: Krebs on Security

item.title: Google accidentally exposed details of unfixed Chromium flaw
item.description: Google has accidentally leaked details about an unfixed issue in Chromium that keeps JavaScript running in the background even when the browser is closed, allowing remote code execution on the device....
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Apple blocked over $11 billion in App Store fraud in 6 years
item.description: Apple revealed that it blocked over $11 billion in fraudulent App Store transactions over the last six years, more than $2.2 billion in potentially fraudulent App Store transactions in 2025 alone. [.....
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Showboat Linux Malware Hits Middle East Telecom with SOCKS5 Proxy Backdoor
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new Linux malware dubbed Showboat that has been put to use in a campaign targeting a telecommunications provider in the Middle East since at least...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Inside a Crypto Drainer: How to Spot it Before it Empties Your Wallet
item.description: Modern crypto drainers don't hack wallets. They trick users into approving malicious transactions. Flare explores how the Lucifer DaaS platform scales wallet theft through phishing and automation. [....
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Chinese hackers target telcos with new Linux, Windows malware
item.description: A Chinese cyber-espionage campaign has been targeting telecommunications providers with newly discovered Linux and Windows malware dubbed Showboat and JFMBackdoor, respectively. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Max severity Cisco Secure Workload flaw gives Site Admin privileges
item.description: Cisco has released security updates to address a maximum-severity vulnerability in Secure Workload that allows attackers to gain Site Admin privileges. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Police seize “First VPN” service used in ransomware, data theft attacks
item.description: A virtual private network service called 'First VPN,' used in ransomware and data theft attacks, has been taken offline in a joint international law enforcement operation. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: Linux Rootkits, Router 0-Day, AI Intrusions, Scam Kits and 25 New Stories
item.description:
This week starts small.
A token leaks. A bad package slips in. A login trick works. An old tool shows up again. At first, it feels like the usual mess. Then you see the pattern: attackers are not alw...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Flipper One project needs community help to build open Linux platform
item.description: Flipper Devices, the maker of the Flipper Zero pentesting tool, is asking the community to help build Flipper One, an open Linux platform for connected devices. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Microsoft Warns of Two Actively Exploited Defender Vulnerabilities
item.description:
Microsoft has disclosed that a privilege escalation and a denial-of-service flaw in Defender has come under active exploitation in the wild.
The former, tracked as CVE-2026-41091, is rated 7.8 on the...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: When Identity is the Attack Path
item.description: Consider a cached access key on a single Windows machine. It got there the way most cached credentials do - a user logged in, and the key stored itself automatically. Standard AWS behavior. No one mis...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Microsoft warns of new Defender zero-days exploited in attacks
item.description: On Wednesday, Microsoft started rolling out security patches for two Defender vulnerabilities that have been exploited in zero-day attacks. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: 9-Year-Old Linux Kernel Flaw Enables Root Command Execution on Major Distros
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a vulnerability in the Linux kernel that remained undetected for nine years.
The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-46333 (CVSS score: 5.5), is a c...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: GitHub Internal Repositories Breached via Malicious Nx Console VS Code Extension
item.description: GitHub on Wednesday officially confirmed that the breach of its internal repositories was the result of a compromise of an employee device involving a poisoned version of the Nx Console Microsoft Visu...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News
